title: Chaos Engineering Practices to Increase Confidence and Reliability
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-13, Reading time: 5 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fengineering.monday.com%2Fchaos-engineering-practices-to-increase-confidence-and-reliability%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019ce6f818bc-a3c366f1-c67a-4727-bba7-e17a32ca768d-000000/NpWPjm-sEmO_XX6eC5ZNOfkEjVpXWNXA7PJkPK-CWLk=448
summary: Chaos Engineering Practices to Increase Confidence and Reliability (5 minute read) Instead of hoping random failures expose weaknesses, the Monday team injects controlled failures in staging, such as network partitions, pod kills, and DNS outages. This practice paid off fast, as what they thought was a working circuit breaker actually hung for 30 seconds and crashed under a simulated network partition, which was caught and fixed the same day.

title: AI should help us produce better code
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-13, Reading time: 5 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fsimonwillison.net%2Fguides%2Fagentic-engineering-patterns%2Fbetter-code%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019ce6f818bc-a3c366f1-c67a-4727-bba7-e17a32ca768d-000000/Xppu6qYF-yW7h2ZFmaGXpS9WSdAkOGUHT4Zj4Oy0avw=448
summary: AI should help us produce better code (5 minute read) While some developers fear AI will degrade code quality, coding agents can actually improve it by proactively addressing technical debt. Agents are great at handling time-consuming refactoring tasks, making it affordable to eliminate minor code smells and architectural inconveniences. Furthermore, AI tools allow developers to explore more options before finalizing designs.

title: The purpose of Continuous Integration is to fail
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-13, Reading time: 6 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fblog.nix-ci.com%2Fpost%2F2026-02-05_the-purpose-of-ci-is-to-fail%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019ce6f818bc-a3c366f1-c67a-4727-bba7-e17a32ca768d-000000/W45TL94rqDkbcWXPXl82o3YbiE10E6GXPdew9MYI05M=448
summary: The purpose of Continuous Integration is to fail (6 minute read) The true purpose and value of Continuous Integration (CI) is in its ability to fail, not to pass. CI acts as a safety net, identifying developer mistakes early in the feedback loop, which prevents faulty code from reaching deployment and incurring greater costs. Conversely, passing CI merely adds overhead without providing new value.

title: “Design Me a Highly Resilient Database”
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-13, Reading time: 9 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fnikogura.com%2FDatabaseDesign.html%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019ce6f818bc-a3c366f1-c67a-4727-bba7-e17a32ca768d-000000/VVA8R_D1ykX87kZW83LCVSxePp124nWZfip6SXs2jgU=448
summary: “Design Me a Highly Resilient Database” (9 minute read) Interview questions that ask candidates to "design a highly resilient database" without providing any context are pointless. For example, database resilience is entirely dependent on specific product requirements, such as data type, consistency needs (e.g., ACID for finance vs. eventual for IoT), and acceptable failure modes. Every database involves inherent tradeoffs.

title: Are LLMs not getting better?
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-13, Reading time: 3 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fentropicthoughts.com%2Fno-swe-bench-improvement%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019ce6f818bc-a3c366f1-c67a-4727-bba7-e17a32ca768d-000000/zolwte7fpPp5C_sgayfmN2wkvSUcv84O0otZSdonsuE=448
summary: Are LLMs not getting better? (3 minute read) LLMs aren't continuously improving their programming abilities necessarily. LLM performance has gotten worse under the stricter criterion compared to the typical benchmarks. Statistical analysis of merge rates since early 2025 suggests a constant or piecewise constant trend, rather than a linear upward slope, indicating a stagnation in capability for over a year.

title: 13-hour AWS outage reportedly caused by Amazon's own AI tools
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-13, Reading time: 4 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.engadget.com%2Fai%2F13-hour-aws-outage-reportedly-caused-by-amazons-own-ai-tools-170930190.html%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019ce6f818bc-a3c366f1-c67a-4727-bba7-e17a32ca768d-000000/6vgPrsvhW_qJ5YHO_Bu_86hj856JH2aUdVvMFPcqXvE=448
summary: 13-hour AWS outage reportedly caused by Amazon's own AI tools (4 minute read) Amazon's own AI coding tool, Kiro, reportedly caused a 13-hour AWS outage in December after it autonomously decided to delete and recreate an environment. However, multiple AWS employees told the Financial Times it was at least the second time Kiro had caused a disruption recently.

title: Fintech's Moats Don't Compile

summary: Fintech's Moats Don't Compile (4 minute read) Fintech's primary moats reside in regulatory permissions, credit data, and risk-bearing capacity rather than replicable software code. Competitive advantage is shifting away from UI toward those who control the "fin" side by managing money movement and proprietary risk models that AI optimizes but cannot replace.

title: Agentic Commerce
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-13, Reading time: 11 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Frobonomics.substack.com%2Fp%2Fagentic-commerce-202603%3Futm_source=tldrai/1/0100019ce759a51c-1768f909-1efc-48cd-8cae-b174603920ed-000000/qNnnsqOMWJBy1BotdXaWzypOjX5U7B0WCvx4WmizZ4o=448
summary: Agentic Commerce (11 minute read) Commerce has historically been closer to a seller's market. Consumers are largely shown what platforms choose to surface. AI agents will gradually shift the power toward the buyer. Instead of navigating the layer of market and platform incentives, AI will enable consumers to access the best product for their needs directly. This dynamic may be most powerful in B2B commerce, which has historically been opaque, fragmented, and relationship-driven.

title: Institutional AI vs Individual AI
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-13, Reading time: 16 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.a16z.news%2Fp%2Finstitutional-ai-vs-individual-ai%3Futm_source=tldrai/1/0100019ce759a51c-1768f909-1efc-48cd-8cae-b174603920ed-000000/l07OBDABFgndIVuML5zqhzMoAWGu1Jyi_OxnQblXIdU=448
summary: Institutional AI vs Individual AI (16 minute read) Institutional AI could unlock far more value than individual AI by redesigning organizations alongside technology. Unlike individual AI, which boosts productivity with little firm value impact, institutional AI focuses on coordination, finding signals in data, countering bias, and scaling revenue. Successful organizations will be those that integrate AI as a foundational component, similar to the industrial shift to electric assembly lines.

title: KIP-1150 Accepted, and the Road Ahead
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-16, Reading time: 8 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Faiven.io%2Fblog%2Fkip-1150-accepted-and-the-road-ahead%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019cf61f57fb-62f7e4b4-02d1-435a-8934-ad48676184f5-000000/rgWgZ4eayRyA8YFyO95g3gQXuClL2s8mdR2Aiq5sn1o=448
summary: KIP-1150 Accepted, and the Road Ahead (8 minute read) KIP-1150's approval brings Diskless Topics to Apache Kafka, enabling compute-storage separation by shifting replication and storage from broker disks to cloud object storage. This transformation slashes total cost of ownership by up to 80%, eliminates inter-AZ replication traffic, and allows for instant elasticity without client changes. This major change positions Kafka as a truly cloud-native streaming standard.

title: Dolt
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-16, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fdolthub%2Fdolt%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019cf61f57fb-62f7e4b4-02d1-435a-8934-ad48676184f5-000000/1v4yYp8KlgwhlVsU7ERM3rNKn5HOGBKsFpRaBzi4u4s=448
summary: Dolt (GitHub Repo) Dolt is an open-source MySQL-compatible database with built-in Git-style version control. It lets you branch, diff, merge, commit, and clone tables and schema just like code in Git, while remaining compatible with standard SQL tools. Useful for data collaboration, reproducibility, auditing, and debugging, you can inspect historical states of datasets and resolve row-level merge conflicts directly in SQL.

title: Can LLMs Be Computers?
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-16, Reading time: 20 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.percepta.ai%2Fblog%2Fcan-llms-be-computers%3Futm_source=tldrai/1/0100019cf6d18831-ebabc6e9-0e4d-4b23-969a-ad2f2a18c962-000000/qsX1lafOnPZ9bsFQ0TJ4pM1MJTXN2Aek5S5GgoPYkAA=448
summary: Can LLMs Be Computers? (20 minute read) Transformers can execute programs efficiently inside their own inference loop. This opens a path towards AI systems that integrate learned representations with compiled algorithms inside a single computational substrate. Solving humanity's toughest problems will require systems that can both reason flexibly and compute reliably. Future AI systems will have software as part of the model.

title: MCP is Dead; Long Live MCP!
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-16, Reading time: 20 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fchrlschn.dev%2Fblog%2F2026%2F03%2Fmcp-is-dead-long-live-mcp%2F%3Futm_source=tldrai/1/0100019cf6d18831-ebabc6e9-0e4d-4b23-969a-ad2f2a18c962-000000/3J9Dr-Wfz2jjC-bcbwamI80FBVWu4bzEPGK-klUrI68=448
summary: MCP is Dead; Long Live MCP! (20 minute read) The current industry zeitgeist is dialed in on CLIs, just like it was on MCP a few short months ago. Using CLI can result in token savings, but custom CLIs run into the same context problems as MCP, except without structure and many other sacrifices. Individual usage of coding agents looks very different from organizational adoption of coding agents. MCP is the present and future for enterprise and org-level use cases.

title: The Five Categories of World Models
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-16, Reading time: 5 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flinks.tldrnewsletter.com%2Fo8t62B/1/0100019cf6d18831-ebabc6e9-0e4d-4b23-969a-ad2f2a18c962-000000/abASZeNbHVge709eg4aC-vAB5Qp62M6Ar0bCLhULjAs=448
summary: The Five Categories of World Models (5 minute read) AMI Labs and World Labs both raised over $1B on "world models," but the term covers five distinct approaches: JEPA, spatial intelligence, learned simulation, physical AI infrastructure, and active inference. The sharpest result in the piece is V-JEPA 2, achieving zero-shot robot planning after training on just 62 hours of domain-specific data. Each approach solves a different subproblem, and the lines between them are expected to blur fast.

title: Your MCP Server Is Eating Your Context Window. There's a Simpler Way
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-17, Reading time: 19 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.apideck.com%2Fblog%2Fmcp-server-eating-context-window-cli-alternative%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019cfb7cbcd1-957fb22b-f8c5-4c9e-940e-c55e6733d692-000000/ipp_2k75H5Ji0lYcONNPis6ZpZFV0ySj78iExw4DrJc=449
summary: Your MCP Server Is Eating Your Context Window. There's a Simpler Way (19 minute read) AI agents using MCP often have context bloat, where extensive tool definitions consume tens of thousands of tokens. In these cases, giving your agent a CLI instead is easier, as it allows for progressive discovery of capabilities through `--help` commands. This CLI approach reduces token consumption and improves reliability by running locally, but is worse at org-wide authentication.
